items.briefing:
The Asia Pacific region is now the top target for financial cyberattacks globally, meaning Australian organisations — including training providers — face heightened risk. Separately, a new report shows that attackers are increasingly exploiting software vulnerabilities rather than tricking people through phishing, so keeping systems and devices updated is more important than ever.

For Australian farmers and rural businesses, new data shows only one in four are considering cyber insurance, leaving most exposed if an incident occurs. If your organisation works with agricultural clients or partners, it's worth raising this gap.

**Practical tip:** Ensure all staff devices and software are set to install updates automatically — patching known vulnerabilities is one of the most effective defences available.

featured.title: WFI says only a quarter of Australian farmers are considering cyber insurance
featured.quote: Australian agricultural sector faces cyber risks with minimal insurance adoption among farmers.
